title: Shared field definitions
description: Domain-neutral, reusable $defs shared by manifest, invoke, completion event, operation status, and callback-response schemas. This document defines no top-level schema of its own; it is only ever consumed via $ref into individual $defs entries so that container schemas (which declare additionalProperties: false) remain self-contained and unambiguous.

contractVersion.description: Wire protocol version. 1 = synchronous-only contract. 2 = adds the deferred completion protocol (callback events, operation status polling, browser continuation).

eventId.description: Opaque, plugin-assigned identifier for a single completion event. Used for callback idempotency.

operationId.description: Opaque, plugin-assigned identifier for a deferred operation.

invocationId.description: Opaque, host-assigned identifier for a single invoke call.

pluginId.description: Opaque identifier for a plugin, unique within the host's plugin registry.

projectId.description: Opaque, host-assigned identifier for the project a tree belongs to. Always present on a v1/v2 invoke request (docs/plugin-system/phase1-implementation.md §6.2: 'project_id — 树所属项目 ID（v1 兼容扩展，恒有）').

actionId.description: Opaque identifier for a single action declared in a plugin manifest, unique within that plugin.

semver.description: Semantic version string for a plugin's own release, independent of contract_version.

sequenceNonNegative.description: Monotonically increasing counter scoped to one operation_id. Non-negative so that a synchronous initial awaiting_user operation status snapshot may report 0 before any callback has been delivered.

pluginState.description: Plugin-reportable state. Host-only states (pending, ok, expired) are never valid here; they exist solely in the host's internal bookkeeping and MUST NOT appear in any plugin-authored event or status body.

pluginStateAccepted.description: Subset of pluginState a plugin may report synchronously in the invoke-accepted response for a deferred action; terminal states cannot be known that early.

pluginStateTerminal.description: Subset of pluginState representing terminal outcomes. Used by the v1 synchronous invoke response, which always completes immediately and therefore can never report awaiting_user or running.

sequencePositive.description: Monotonically increasing counter scoped to one operation_id, strictly positive. Used by completion events delivered over the callback: sequence 0 is reserved for the synchronous, non-callback initial operation status snapshot (see sequenceNonNegative) and is never sent as a callback event.

resultMode.description: The execution environment the plugin ran this operation in — NOT a result-transport mode. 'mock' = a non-production/sandbox/test execution (no real side effects against production downstream systems). 'production' = a genuine production execution. This is completely independent of whether/how a result artifact is delivered: result_url (see the httpsUrl-typed result_url property on each schema that carries one) is always independently optional and MUST NOT be conditionally required or forbidden based on this field's value.

occurredAt.description: RFC 3339 timestamp of when the reported state transition occurred, as observed by the plugin.

progress.description: Optional coarse progress indicator. additionalProperties is false: this object carries no domain-specific sub-fields.

externalId.description: Opaque identifier assigned by an external system the plugin delegates work to (e.g. a job queue). Printable, non-whitespace ASCII only; domain-neutral and provider-agnostic.

provider.description: Opaque, conservative-ASCII label identifying the downstream provider/subsystem that produced the event, if any.

errorMessage.description: Human-readable error detail. Not machine-parsed; no character-set restriction beyond length.

httpsUrl.description: Absolute HTTPS URL, e.g. result_url or launch_url.

epochSeconds.description: NumericDate as defined by RFC 7519 (JWT): a JSON number representing seconds since 1970-01-01T00:00:00Z UTC, ignoring leap seconds.

jwtId.description: Opaque, single-use identifier for one browser continuation assertion (JWT 'jti' claim). MUST be consumed (rejected on reuse) exactly once by the verifying party.

treeId.description: Opaque, host-assigned identifier for the content tree a browser continuation assertion is scoped to. Domain-neutral: this repository imposes no meaning on the identifier beyond opacity and uniqueness within the host.

assertionPurpose.description: Fixed audience-confusion guard for the browser continuation interaction-assertion JWT. This claim's value never varies; its presence and constant value let a verifier reject tokens minted for any other purpose even if they otherwise share the signing key, issuer, and audience.

csrfToken.description: Opaque, single-use-session-scoped anti-CSRF token issued alongside the browser continuation session cookie. Callers MUST echo it back in the X-ContentTree-CSRF-Token header on subsequent state-changing requests within that browser session.

jwtCompact.description: A JWS Compact Serialization string (RFC 7515): three base64url segments separated by '.'. Used for the browser continuation interaction-assertion, always RS256-signed.

browserAssertionHandoffLocation.description: Final HTTPS Location value for browser continuation. It carries exactly one fragment field named contenttree_assertion whose value is a JWS Compact Serialization. No assertion is permitted in the path or query, and no other fragment field is permitted. The relationship that the prefix before '#' exactly preserves the plugin-provided, fragment-free launch_url is a construction invariant described in docs/protocol.md.

hostApiBaseUrl.description: Absolute origin (scheme + host[:port], no path/query/fragment) at which the host exposes its plugin-host API for this invocation, so the plugin never has to guess or construct the callback destination itself (anti-SSRF). HTTPS is required except for the explicit http://localhost / http://127.0.0.1 allowance, which exists solely to support local conformance testing and MUST NOT be honored by production hosts/plugins.

hostApiRoutePrefix.description: Optional normalized absolute path prefix under which a gateway mounts the host API, for example '/NewContentTree'. It starts with exactly one '/', contains one or more non-empty RFC 3986 pchar segments without percent encoding, and has no trailing slash, query, fragment, backslash, '.' segment, '..' segment, scheme, or authority. Omit this field when the host API is mounted at the origin root. Plugins construct callback URLs as {host_api_base_url}{host_api_route_prefix or ''}/api/plugin-host/..., without reinterpreting or normalizing either component.
hostApiRoutePrefix.$comment: The final negative lookahead is an ECMA-262-compatible exact end-of-input assertion; '$' alone also matches immediately before a final line terminator.

id.description: Host-assigned identifier for the invoking user. Always present, regardless of granted scopes.

username.description: Present only when the effective scopes for this invocation include user:identity:basic.

corp_id.description: Present only when the effective scopes for this invocation include user:identity:basic.

wechat_userid.description: Present only when the effective scopes for this invocation include user:identity:basic.

mobile.description: Present only when the effective scopes for this invocation include user:contact:mobile.

invokeUser.description: Invoking user identity, data-minimized per the manifest's declared/granted scopes (docs/plugin-system/README.md §6.3). 'id' is unconditionally required; every other property is scope-gated and therefore optional at the schema level.

invokeSource.description: Identifies which snapshot/run the exported tree was resolved from (docs/plugin-system/README.md §7; routers/export.py::_resolve_export_tree semantics). Both keys are always present but individually nullable: null means 'not resolved from a specific snapshot/run' (e.g. the tree's current live state).

treeExport.description: The exported content tree, produced by the host's own export serialization (docs/plugin-system/README.md §7: 'export_service.py::export_json 的输出结构'). This object is intentionally NOT closed (no additionalProperties: false): the host's export shape is allowed to gain additional fields across releases without that alone being an incompatible contract change, per the documented evolution policy ('同一大版本内只增字段不删改' — within one contract_version, only add fields, never remove/rename). This repository does not attempt to exhaustively enumerate or freeze every field of the export format.

invokeSkin.description: Optional skin-usage metadata, present only when the invoked action's manifest input_contract declares include_skin_html: true and a matching skin usage/run exists; null when no such artifact exists (this is a documented silent-degrade case, never a validation failure). Like treeExport, this object is intentionally not closed to allow additive evolution of its generic fields (e.g. usage_id, run_id, matched_nodes, total_nodes — docs/plugin-system/phase1-implementation.md §6.2).

invokeOptions.description: Opaque, action-specific options collected by the host's own confirmation UI (docs/plugin-system/README.md §7). Domain-neutral and intentionally unconstrained in shape; this repository imposes no schema on its contents.

endpointPath.description: A path (not an absolute URL) rooted at the plugin's own base_url, e.g. '/actions' or '/operations/{operation_id}'. Manifest endpoints are always advertised as paths; the host resolves them by concatenating its out-of-band-registered plugin base_url with this path (and, for the invoke endpoint, a further '/{action_id}' segment).

i18nMap.description: Optional BCP-47-ish locale-code -> human-readable string map (e.g. {"zh-CN": "...", "en-US": "..."}), used for the manifest's own name/label localization. Never authoritative for scopes/permissions.

scopeName.description: One documented, domain-neutral permission scope a plugin manifest may declare (docs/plugin-system/phase1-implementation.md §4.2). Closed set: hosts reject any scope not in this enumeration.
